brief.heading: Where the 2026 path stands
brief.body: The House-passed HJR 203 path is blocked because the proposal died in Senate Appropriations. No tracked property-tax elimination vehicle appeared on the April 28, 2026 Special Session D bill-actions list reviewed for this tracker.

status_legend.label: Red
status_legend.meaning: Blocked, dead, or not currently moving

monthly_runway.heading: Current Path To Ballot
monthly_runway.scale_note: Monthly scale; future months are checkpoints to watch, not predictions.
monthly_runway.next_watch: New special session call, new HJR or SJR filing, committee agenda, or ballot record

months.title: House vehicle filed
months.summary: HJR 203 entered the 2026 regular-session bill record.

months.title: House passed; Senate received
months.summary: CS/CS/HJR 203 passed the House and was referred to Senate Appropriations.

months.title: No Special Session D vehicle found
months.summary: The reviewed bill-actions list did not show a tracked property-tax elimination proposal.

months.title: Possible new legislative vehicle
months.summary: A future special-session call, HJR, or SJR would need official source support before this tracker marks a vehicle active.

months.title: Ballot and voter approval checkpoints
months.summary: A constitutional-amendment path would still require voter approval before any constitutional change.

path_steps.label: Filed
path_steps.title: HJR 203 entered the House record
path_steps.description: The House property-tax proposal that became CS/CS/HJR 203 was filed for the 2026 regular session.
path_steps.dependency: This created the primary House vehicle tracked in the elimination debate.

path_steps.label: House
path_steps.title: House approval completed
path_steps.description: CS/CS/HJR 203 passed the Florida House by an 80-30 vote.
path_steps.dependency: A legislative constitutional amendment also needed Senate approval before any ballot step.

path_steps.label: Senate
path_steps.title: Senate path blocked
path_steps.dependency: This ended the regular-session path for that specific House proposal.

path_steps.label: Vehicle
path_steps.title: No Special Session D vehicle identified
path_steps.description: No tracked property-tax elimination proposal appeared on the April 28, 2026 Special Session D bill-actions list reviewed for this tracker.
path_steps.dependency: A new special session call, HJR, or SJR would be needed to reopen a legislative route.

path_steps.label: Ballot
path_steps.title: Ballot listing not reached
path_steps.description: A legislative constitutional amendment would still need voter approval before it could change the Florida Constitution.
path_steps.dependency: The Department of State ballot step comes after a measure advances through the required constitutional-amendment path.

next_panels.label: What changed
next_panels.body: HJR 203 passed one chamber but died in Senate Appropriations during the regular session.

next_panels.label: What to watch
next_panels.body: Watch for a new special session proclamation, new HJR or SJR filing, committee agenda, or ballot record.

next_panels.label: Data depth
next_panels.body: County comparisons stay separate from the overview until a proposal version, source type, and verification date are available.

known_unknowns: No active tracked elimination vehicle is shown in the current tracker data.
A future proposal may differ from CS/CS/HJR 203 and should be tracked as a separate vehicle.
County-impact comparisons need version-labeled sources before they should be treated as comparable.

depth_links.title: Details
depth_links.body: Read the source-backed legislative dossier, claims, proposal history, and full timeline.
depth_links.label: Secondary view

depth_links.title: Timeline
depth_links.body: Follow the chronological record and dependencies behind each procedural checkpoint.
depth_links.label: Global view

depth_links.title: Counties
depth_links.body: Use the advanced county table once version-labeled impact estimates are available.
depth_links.label: Advanced view
